changed_functions.name: GpiopGetBaseRegistrationBufferSize
key_changes: Return value changed from 0x68 to 0x70 (mov eax, 0x68 -> mov eax, 0x70). This constant is the base allocation size for the PO_FX_DEVICE registration buffer and grows by 8 bytes to match the enlarged structure layout.
Missed by structure-only comparison because only the immediate operand changed, not the opcode/register structure.
changed_functions.security_notes: The base registration-buffer size grew by 8 bytes (0x68 -> 0x70), consistent with a new DWORD field being inserted into the PO_FX_DEVICE layout. Whether the pre-patch smaller/differently-laid-out buffer produced any misbehavior depends on the kernel's PoFxRegisterPrimaryDevice parser, which is not part of these binaries. No attacker-controlled input reaches this size; it is a fixed constant. Not scored as a security fix.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
GpiopGetBaseRegistrationBufferSize @ 0x1c0013c14 (UNPATCHED):
  00000001C0013C14  mov     eax, 68h
  00000001C0013C19  retn

GpiopGetBaseRegistrationBufferSize @ 0x1c0013c14 (PATCHED):
  00000001C0013C14  mov     eax, 70h
  00000001C0013C19  retn

changed_functions.name: GpiopInitializePrimaryDevice
key_changes: New DWORD field added at offset 0x08 of the PO_FX_DEVICE registration buffer, initialized to 1.

All six callback function pointers shifted +8 bytes: ActiveConditionCallback 0x08->0x10, IdleConditionCallback 0x10->0x18, IdleStateCallback 0x18->0x20, PowerRequiredCallback 0x20->0x28, PowerNotRequiredCallback 0x28->0x30, CriticalTransitionCallback 0x38->0x40.
Zeroed field shifted 0x30->0x38; DeviceContext shifted 0x40->0x48. Both builds write version=1 at offset 0x00 and the component count at offset 0x04.
changed_functions.security_notes: The function fills the PO_FX_DEVICE buffer with the same callback targets, component count, and device-context pointer in both builds; only the field offsets and the extra DWORD at 0x08 differ. The value at offset 0x00 (the structure version field passed to PoFxRegisterPrimaryDevice) is 1 in both builds. The buffer contents are fixed at compile time; no IOCTL or other attacker-controlled input reaches these fields. Any consequence of the layout difference is a property of the out-of-scope kernel PoFx parser, so no reachable primitive is demonstrable from the driver binaries. The change is consistent with a recompile against an updated PO_FX_DEVICE definition (coordinated servicing) and is not scored as a security fix.

  00000001C0013C7C  mov     [rcx+28h], rax

changed_functions.name: GpiopInitializeIdleStateBufferAndCount
key_changes: Per-component IdleStates pointer moved from (i+3)*32 to i*32+0x68, matching the enlarged component-array base.
Per-component IdleStateCount moved from i*32+0x58 to (i+3)*32.
Register allocation changed as a side effect of the reordering (r10 now holds comp_idx*32).
changed_functions.security_notes: The same count and pointer values are written in both builds; only the destination offsets changed to track the enlarged registration-structure layout. No attacker-controlled input reaches these fields. Consistent with a recompile against an updated PO_FX_DEVICE/PO_FX_COMPONENT definition; not scored as a security fix.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
GpiopInitializeIdleStateBufferAndCount @ 0x1c0013c20 (UNPATCHED):
  00000001C0013C20  mov     eax, edx
  00000001C0013C22  shl     rax, 5
  00000001C0013C26  mov     r10d, edx
  00000001C0013C29  mov     [rax+rcx+58h], r9d
  00000001C0013C2E  lea     rax, [r10+3]
  00000001C0013C32  shl     rax, 5
  00000001C0013C36  mov     [rax+rcx], r8
  00000001C0013C3A  retn

GpiopInitializeIdleStateBufferAndCount @ 0x1c0013c20 (PATCHED):

  00000001C0013C23  lea     rax, [r10+3]
  00000001C0013C27  shl     r10, 5

  00000001C0013C2F  mov     [r10+rcx+68h], r8
  00000001C0013C34  mov     [rax+rcx], r9d

security_findings.entry_point: GpioClxEvtDeviceSelfManagedIoInit (WDF EvtDeviceSelfManagedIoInit callback) — runs during GPIO controller PnP device start
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change — PoFx registration structure layout/version update
security_findings.description: The patch enlarges the PO_FX_DEVICE power-framework registration buffer by 8 bytes (GpiopGetBaseRegistrationBufferSize 0x68 -> 0x70), inserts a new DWORD field (initialized to 1) at offset 0x08, and shifts every subsequent field down by 8 bytes (six callback pointers and the DeviceContext), with matching per-component offset adjustments in GpiopInitializeIdleStateBufferAndCount. Both builds write the same version value (1) at offset 0x00 and the same component count at offset 0x04, and fill the buffer with the same fixed compile-time callback addresses and device-context pointer. No attacker-controlled input reaches the changed fields. The security consequence of the layout difference, if any, is a property of the kernel's PoFxRegisterPrimaryDevice parser, which is not part of these binaries and cannot be evaluated from a driver-to-driver diff. The change is consistent with the driver being recompiled against an updated PO_FX_DEVICE structure definition (coordinated servicing) and is not scored as a security fix. The earlier characterization as a V1/V3 type-confusion leading to kernel memory corruption is not supported: the driver stamps the same version value in both builds, and the specific corruption arithmetic previously cited (a callback pointer's low DWORD read as ~19 million idle states) is incorrect — the low 32 bits of the callback address 0x1c0013020 are 0xc0013020, and no such kernel misread is demonstrable from these binaries.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: The registration path runs during ordinary GPIO controller device start (GpioClxEvtDeviceSelfManagedIoInit -> GpiopRegisterWithPowerFramework -> PoFxRegisterPrimaryDevice). The buffer contents are fixed at compile time; no IOCTL or other attacker-controlled data reaches the changed fields. Whether the layout difference between builds causes any misbehavior depends on the out-of-scope kernel PoFx parser.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrable from the driver binaries. Inputs to the changed functions are the buffer pointer, fixed compile-time callback addresses, the component count, and the device-context pointer — none attacker-tunable.

  00000001C0013C66  mov     [rcx+10h], rax
  00000001C0013C6A  lea     rax, GpioClxDevicePowerRequiredCallback
  00000001C0013C71  mov     [rcx+20h], rax
  00000001C0013C75  lea     rax, GpioClxDevicePowerNotRequiredCallback

  00000001C0013C94  mov     dword ptr [rcx], 1
  00000001C0013C9A  mov     [rcx+4], edx

=== GpiopInitializeIdleStateBufferAndCount @ 0x1c0013c20 (UNPATCHED) ===
  00000001C0013C20  mov     eax, edx

=== GpiopRegisterWithPowerFramework @ 0x1c0025b10 (allocation sequence, identical in both builds) ===
  00000001C0025B28  movzx   r9d, word ptr [rcx+2D8h]
  00000001C0025B33  call    GpiopGetBaseRegistrationBufferSize
  00000001C0025B3E  shl     edx, 5
  00000001C0025B48  lea     edi, [rdx-19h]
  00000001C0025B4F  add     edi, eax
  00000001C0025B51  and     edi, 0FFFFFFF8h
  00000001C0025B8A  lea     ecx, [rdi+rax*8]
  00000001C0025B94  call    ExAllocatePoolWithTag
  00000001C0025C03  call    GpiopInitializePrimaryDevice
  00000001C0025C3F  call    GpiopInitializeIdleStateBufferAndCount
  00000001C0025CB6  call    GpiopRegisterPrimaryDeviceInternal
security_findings.notes: An independent content diff of both builds confirms the change is confined to these three functions; no other function differs, and no new sanitizer, guard, or mitigation was introduced. Establishing any reachable security impact would require the specific kernel build's PoFxRegisterPrimaryDevice parser, which is out of scope for a driver-to-driver diff.

summary: The patch updates the layout of msgpioclx.sys's PO_FX_DEVICE power-framework registration buffer: the base allocation size grows from 0x68 to 0x70, a new DWORD field (=1) is inserted at offset 0x08, and every subsequent field (six callback pointers and the DeviceContext) shifts down by 8 bytes, with matching per-component offset adjustments. Three functions changed: GpiopGetBaseRegistrationBufferSize, GpiopInitializePrimaryDevice, and GpiopInitializeIdleStateBufferAndCount. Both builds write the same version value (1) at offset 0x00 and fill the buffer with the same fixed compile-time callback addresses, component count, and device-context pointer; no attacker-controlled input reaches the changed fields. Any consequence of the layout difference is a property of the out-of-scope kernel PoFxRegisterPrimaryDevice parser and is not demonstrable from these driver binaries. The change is treated as a coordinated structure-version update (recompile against an updated definition), not a delivered security fix. No severity or CWE is assigned.
